text.title: Simulation Workflow and Prompt Patterns
text.summary: Subagent patterns for persona simulation and interview analysis, prompt references, and agent gotchas.

section.title: Agent Workflow
section.p: Claude subagents can simulate synthetic user interviews directly: given a persona definition and a discussion guide, the agent responds as the simulated user across multiple questions. A second agent (researcher role) then analyzes the synthetic interview transcripts for themes. The loop is: human defines persona + study → agent plays user → agent analyzes → human validates key findings with at least 3 real users. Never skip the real-user validation gate.

list.item: sonnet — synthetic user simulation (requires nuanced persona adherence), interview analysis, theme extraction
haiku — persona attribute generation, quantitative response scoring (Likert-scale surveys at scale)

rule.statement: Use temperature 0.7–0.9 for synthetic user simulation. Temperature 0 makes responses too consistent — all personas converge to the same answers.
rule.rationale: Realistic variation between synthetic users requires non-zero temperature; without it, running 5 personas produces the same output 5 times, which is not directionally useful.

section.title: Prompt and Template References
section.reference: Run multi-persona synthetic interview panel via Claude API
System prompt for synthetic user interview simulation
Prompt for extracting themes from synthetic transcripts with confidence flags

antipattern.description: Agent playing a synthetic user breaks character when probed on technical details — answers as AI, not as persona.
antipattern.reason: LLM simulation degrades under probing; deep technical questions reveal the AI substrate; these responses are not valid as synthetic user data.

antipattern.description: Synthetic users display implausible product knowledge in simulation.
antipattern.reason: AI models are trained on product descriptions on the internet; synthetic personas know more about your product than a real user would. Prime explicitly: instruct the persona to have limited product awareness.

antipattern.description: Agent-analyzed themes from synthetic interviews lack evidence quotes.
antipattern.reason: Synthetic themes have double hallucination risk (simulation + analysis); require evidence quotes for every extracted theme to surface fabrication.
